faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Vehicle Import
faq.q1: Do I need an EUR.1 form?
faq.a1: If you're importing a vehicle from the EU or an FTA country, the EUR.1 form (or supplier's declaration) enables duty-free import. The seller/dealer issues it.
faq.a2: When moving to Switzerland and bringing your car, you can import it tax-free as household effects (Form 18.44) — provided you've owned it for at least 6 months.
faq.q3: Do I have to pay CO₂ sanctions?
faq.a3: Only if the vehicle was registered abroad for less than 6 months (or less than 12 months with under 5,000 km). Older used vehicles are generally exempt.
faq.a4: VAT (8.1%) is calculated on the total: purchase price + customs duty + automobile tax + transport costs to the Swiss border.
faq.a5: Form 13.20A is the customs clearance certificate. It is ISSUED by customs — you don't fill it in yourself. It's needed for cantonal vehicle registration.
faq.q6: Where can I find my vehicle's empty weight?
faq.a6: In the vehicle registration certificate (Zulassungsbescheinigung Teil I), the COC (Certificate of Conformity), or on the manufacturer's website.
